picture.tex: \hbox to338.1pt{\vbox to232.93pt{\pgfpicture\makeatletter\hbox{\thinspace% \lower 0.0pt\hbox to0.0pt{\pgfsys@beginscope\pgfsys@invoke{ }\definecolor{% pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0,0,0}\pgfsys@color@rgb@stroke{0}{0}{0}\pgfsys@invoke{ }% \pgfsys@color@rgb@fill{0}{0}{0}\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@setlinewidth{\the% \pgflinewidth}\pgfsys@invoke{ }\nullfont\hbox to0.0pt{\pgfsys@beginscope% \pgfsys@invoke{ }{}{}{}{}\pgfsys@beginscope\pgfsys@invoke{ }{}{}{}{}{}{}{}{}% \definecolor[named]{pgffillcolor}{rgb}{0.7,0.7,0.7}\pgfsys@color@gray@fill{0.7% }\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@fill@opacity{1.0}\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@moveto{0.0% pt}{0.0pt}\pgfsys@lineto{0.0pt}{232.92714pt}\pgfsys@lineto{338.09853pt}{232.92% 714pt}\pgfsys@lineto{338.09853pt}{0.0pt}\pgfsys@closepath\pgfsys@fill% \pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@endscope\pgfsys@beginscope% \pgfsys@invoke{ }{}{}{}{}{}{}{}{}\definecolor[named]{pgffillcolor}{rgb}{% 0.98,0.98,0.98}\pgfsys@color@gray@fill{0.98}\pgfsys@invoke{ }% \pgfsys@fill@opacity{1.0}\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@moveto{0.4pt}{0.4pt}% \pgfsys@lineto{0.4pt}{232.52715pt}\pgfsys@lineto{337.69853pt}{232.52715pt}% \pgfsys@lineto{337.69853pt}{0.4pt}\pgfsys@closepath\pgfsys@fill\pgfsys@invoke{% }\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@endscope\pgfsys@beginscope\pgfsys@invoke{ }% \pgfsys@fill@opacity{1.0}\pgfsys@invoke{ }{{{}}{{}}{{}}{{}}{{}}{{}}{{}}% \pgfsys@beginscope\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@transformcm{1.0}{0.0}{0.0}{1.0}{14.% 62634pt}{10.93579pt}\pgfsys@invoke{ }\hbox{{\color[rgb]{0,0,0}\definecolor[% named]{pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0,0,0}\pgfsys@color@gray@stroke{0}% \pgfsys@color@gray@fill{0}\hbox{\minipage[b]{308.84584pt}\color[rgb]{0,0,0}% \definecolor[named]{pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0,0,0}\pgfsys@color@gray@stroke{0}% \pgfsys@color@gray@fill{0}\ignorespaces\ignorespaces\footnotesize The current % kernel runs the 256$\times$256 loop fully sequentially ($\sim$65{,}543 cycles)% , so there is still headroom. To slash the latency you should: \begin{enumerate}\par\enumerate@item@Hoist {B[i]} load out of the inner j-loop% so you only read B once per row. \par\enumerate@item@Pipeline the inner loop with II=1 (\#pragma HLS pipeline % II=1). \par\enumerate@item@Vectorize by a factor of 4 on a 128-bit data path: pack 4 % values into one AXI transfer, unroll the inner loop by 4, and operate on 4 % elements/cycle to get a 4$\times$ speedup. \par\enumerate@item@(Optional) Separate memory-load, compute and write into % three functions and apply \#pragma HLS dataflow to overlap A/B reads, compute,% and C writes. \par\end{enumerate}\par With these changes you can reduce the cycles from $% \sim$65{,}536 to $\sim$16{,}384 (plus a small pipeline fill/drain), cutting % your latency by 4$\times$. \endminipage}}}\pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@endscope}\pgfsys@invoke{ }% \pgfsys@endscope \pgfsys@invoke{ }\pgfsys@endscope{}{}{}\hss}\pgfsys@discardpath\pgfsys@invoke{% }\pgfsys@endscope\hss}}\endpgfpicture}}

p.text:
The current kernel runs the 256
×
256 loop fully sequentially (
∼
65,543 cycles), so there is still headroom. To slash the latency you should:
load out of the inner j-loop so you only read B once per row.
Pipeline the inner loop with II=1 (#pragma HLS pipeline II=1).
Vectorize by a factor of 4 on a 128-bit data path: pack 4 values into one AXI transfer, unroll the inner loop by 4, and operate on 4 elements/cycle to get a 4
(Optional) Separate memory-load, compute and write into three functions and apply #pragma HLS dataflow to overlap A/B reads, compute, and C writes.

With these changes you can reduce the cycles from
∼
65,536 to
∼
16,384 (plus a small pipeline fill/drain), cutting your latency by 4
×
.

figure.toccaption: 1Feedback produced by the judge agent highlighting optimization opportunities.
figure.caption: Figure 1Feedback produced by the judge agent highlighting optimization opportunities.
